analysis.summary: Moga+Sanmitro is favoured (42.6% win vs 37.3% for Ryan, 20.2% draw). Expected goals: Moga+Sanmitro 2.24 – 2.13 Ryan.

key_factors.explanation: Moga+Sanmitro is more exposed on the counter (transition risk 0.14 vs 0.46 — lower is safer).
Moga+Sanmitro has the stronger keeper (0.45 vs 0.33), shifting expected goals against.
Moga+Sanmitro can disrupt the other's build-up (press intensity vs press-resistance edge -0.10).
Ryan defends better in the back line (0.47 vs 0.53).
Moga+Sanmitro shields the defence better (mid-def 0.33 vs 0.28).

sections.title: Verdict
paragraphs: Moga+Sanmitro is favoured (42.6% win vs 37.3% for Ryan, 20.2% draw). Expected goals: Moga+Sanmitro 2.24 – 2.13 Ryan.
bullets: Moga+Sanmitro is more exposed on the counter (transition risk 0.14 vs 0.46 — lower is safer).
Moga+Sanmitro has the stronger keeper (0.45 vs 0.33), shifting expected goals against.

sections.title: Season profiles (prime / pick-season)
paragraphs: Moga+Sanmitro pick-season: Ángel Di María uses 2013-2014 only (stats fully replaced).
Ryan pick-season: Neymar uses 2014-2015 only (stats fully replaced).
bullets: Prime player: entire stat line replaced by their best top-league season (2014-15+).
Pick-season player: stats replaced by the chosen season only (not blended with current form).

sections.title: Squad depth (bench impact)
paragraphs: Non-starters with elite per-90 traits add a small squad-depth multiplier to unit ratings (capped at ~5% total; bench cannot dominate outcomes).
Moga+Sanmitro: 4 on bench; 3 with standout quality (+4.5% squad depth boost). Standouts: Fabián Ruiz, Anthony Gordon, Benjamin Šeško. Boosts — attack +1.9%, creation +1.8%, defence +0.8%.
Ryan: 4 on bench; 3 with standout quality (+4.9% squad depth boost). Standouts: Neymar, Warren Zaïre-Emery, Piero Hincapié. Boosts — attack +2.0%, creation +1.5%, defence +1.5%.

sections.title: Expected goals pipeline
paragraphs: The engine splits attack into finishing and chance-creation channels (xA / xG-buildup / xG-chain credit all outfield positions), then applies opponent suppression (back line 54%, mid shield 32%, GK 14%) and midfield battle modifiers.
Moga+Sanmitro raw attack xG: 2.65 finishing + 0.23 creation = 2.88 before suppression. When Ryan defends, suppression factor is 0.819 (DEF 0.53, mid-def 0.28, GK 0.33, transition risk 0.46).
Ryan raw attack xG: 2.67 + 0.18 = 2.85. Moga+Sanmitro suppression when defending: 0.843 (transition risk 0.14).
Midfield battle multipliers: Moga+Sanmitro ×1.004, Ryan ×0.996. Net xG edge: Moga+Sanmitro by 0.11.

sections.title: Defensive structure & transitions
paragraphs: Moga+Sanmitro xGA suppression: 0.723 (base without transition penalty: 0.813). Ryan: 0.744 (base 0.821).

Moga+Sanmitro fullbacks: Riccardo Calafiori (RB, exposure 0.16), Jurriën Timber (LB, exposure 0.19). Team transition risk 0.142 — high attacking fullbacks increase counter vulnerability when midfield cover is thin.

Ryan fullbacks: Marcos Llorente (RB, exposure 0.27), Alejandro Grimaldo (LB, exposure 0.52). Team transition risk 0.460 — high attacking fullbacks increase counter vulnerability when midfield cover is thin.
Opposition wing threat vs leaky fullbacks: Moga+Sanmitro's wide attack gets a +2.4% xG edge (winger threat 0.60, Ryan transition risk 0.46).

Press vs build-up: Moga+Sanmitro's press trims Ryan's chance creation by ~9.5% (press 0.45 vs resistance 0.09). Press vs build-up: Ryan's press trims Moga+Sanmitro's chance creation by ~8.2% (press 0.44 vs resistance 0.18).

sections.title: Formation & squad fit
paragraphs: Moga+Sanmitro (4-3-3 flat): average formation fit 0.81. Chance creation index 0.58, possession 0.70.
Ryan (4-3-3 flat): average formation fit 0.82. Chance creation index 0.49, possession 0.68.

sections.title: Monte Carlo interpretation
paragraphs: Across 10,000 neutral-venue runs, average scoreline tendency is 2.25–2.12. Both teams score 79.1% of the time; over 2.5 goals 81.0%.
bullets: Trophy boost: Moga+Sanmitro ×1.022, Ryan ×1.016 (applied in simulation units).
